sections.paragraph: φιλαδελφία, ας, ἡ [φιλάδελφος] in its central sense φ. expresses ‘affection/love for a sibling’; in ext. sense as used in the NT it signifies caring attitude toward members of a fictive kinship group with common interests, namely, fellow believers brotherly [and sisterly] love Ro 12:10; 1 Th 4:9; Hb 13:1; 1 Pt 1:22; 2 Pt 1:7.
